title: Design the React command palette for session actions
summary: Started a command palette concept that would let operators open sessions, jump to conflicts, and filter frontend work without hunting through tabs.
goal: Prototype the next interaction layer on top of the workspace shell so large React session corpora remain operable from the keyboard.
changesMade: Mapped the key session actions that deserve palette commands
Outlined a shared action model for open, compare, filter, and inspect
Identified the UI seams in dashboard.tsx where the palette can anchor
Sketched how sessions_context output can seed searchable command items

features.name: session action palette
features.why: Keyboardable actions become necessary once the frontend tool has dozens of sessions and several panes.

features.name: command item dataset shaping
features.why: Palette search needs a compact feed of titles, statuses, branches, and touched files.

features.name: shell-level interaction hooks
features.why: The palette should feel native to the workspace shell rather than bolted onto one tab.

decisions.decision: Search across session metadata first, raw patch content later
decisions.why: Palette interactions should stay fast and relevant before widening into full-text search.

decisions.decision: Let the palette launch existing views instead of creating a new page type
decisions.why: The shell already has the right panes; the palette should be navigation and command glue.

blockers: Need a shared action state model before palette execution can open or focus a pane reliably.
openQuestions: Should palette commands expose raw session JSON and patch paths directly for power users?
Do we want one global palette or separate palettes scoped to sessions and conflicts?
resumePrompt: Continue the command palette by stabilizing the action model in dashboard/src/lib/data.ts and then threading palette entry points through dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx.
